perturbation.original_text: The term "Evaluation Material" does not include any information which (i) at the time of disclosure or thereafter is generally available to and known by the public (other than in the case of Evaluation Material for Scientific Games as a result of a disclosure directly or indirectly by MDI or its Representatives and in the case of Evaluation Material for MDI as a result of a disclosure directly or indirectly by Scientific Games or its Representatives), (ii) was or becomes available to Scientific Games or MDI on a nonconfidential basis from a source other than the other party or its advisors, provided that such source is not and was not bound by a confidentiality agreement with or for the benefit of the party with respect to whom the information pertains, or (iii) was in the possession of the disclosing party prior to the date of this Agreement and was obtained by such party without the breach, directly or indirectly, by such party or any other person of any obligation or duty owed to the party with respect to whom the information pertains.
perturbation.changed_text: The term "Evaluation Material" includes all information exchanged between the parties, regardless of whether it is already publicly available or was previously known to either party. This includes information disclosed directly or indirectly by either party or its Representatives, even if it was already in the public domain. There are no exceptions to what constitutes 'Evaluation Material'.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation fundamentally alters the definition of "Evaluation Material." The original text excludes information that is publicly available, received non-confidentially from another source, or already in the disclosing party's possession. The modified text includes ALL information, regardless of its prior status, creating a direct contradiction. Also the text now contradicts with paragraph (3), where If a transaction between the Companies is not consummated or if either party at any time so requests, each party will promptly return to the other party all copies of the Evaluation Material in its possession or in the possession of its Representatives, and each party will destroy all copies of any analyses, compilations, studies or other documents prepared by it or for its use containing or reflecting any Evaluation Material.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If a transaction between the Companies is not consummated or if either party at any time so requests, each party will promptly return to the other party all copies of the Evaluation Material in its possession or in the possession of its Representatives, and each party will destroy all copies of any analyses, compilations, studies or other documents prepared by it or for its use containing or reflecting any Evaluation Material.
perturbation.justification: The changed text redefines 'Evaluation Material' to include all information exchanged, regardless of prior public availability or existing knowledge. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which requires the return or destruction of 'Evaluation Material'. If 'Evaluation Material' now encompasses everything, including publicly available information, the obligation to return/destroy it becomes nonsensical and impossible to fulfill, as it would require destroying information already in the public domain. The original text clearly excludes certain types of information from the definition of 'Evaluation Material', while the changed text explicitly includes everything, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Without the prior written consent of the other party, each party will not, and will cause its Representatives not to, disclose to any person either the status of the investigations, discussions or negotiations taking place concerning a possible transaction between the parties, or that either party has requested or received Evaluation Material from the other party or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect to any such possible transaction except as disclosed in that certain letter of intent between the parties dated February 25, 2002.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party is free to disclose the status of the investigations, discussions, or negotiations taking place concerning a possible transaction between the parties, including the fact that either party has requested or received Evaluation Material from the other party. This disclosure may be made to any person without obtaining prior written consent from the other party. Each party is allowed to disclose this information with no written consent from the other party, contradicting what was stated before.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation completely negates the confidentiality provision regarding the status of investigations and negotiations. The original text strictly prohibits disclosure without prior written consent; the modified text explicitly allows disclosure without consent, creating a clear contradiction. The text also contracts paragraph (1) where the text says 'The Evaluation Material will be used solely for the purpose of evaluating a possible transaction between the Companies and not in any way directly or indirectly detrimental to the Companies (competitively or otherwise), and unless and until the parties have completed a transaction pursuant to a formal contractually binding agreement ("Definitive Agreement"), such information will be kept confidential'
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Evaluation Material will be used solely for the purpose of evaluating a possible transaction between the Companies and not in any way directly or indirectly detrimental to the Companies (competitively or otherwise), and unless and until the parties have completed a transaction pursuant to a formal contractually binding agreement ("Definitive Agreement"), such information will be kept confidential, except that each party may disclose the Evaluation Material or portions thereof to those of its directors, officers, employees, consultants, advisors and professional representatives (the persons to whom such disclosure is permissible being collectively called "Representatives") in each case who need to know such information for the purpose of evaluating a possible transaction between the Companies; provided , however , that prior to disclosing the Evaluation Material or any portion thereof to any of such Representatives, the disclosing party will secure the undertaking of its Representative to be bound by the terms of this Confidentiality Agreement to the same extent that the disclosing party is bound by this Agreement. Each party agrees to be responsible for any breach of this Agreement or such undertaking by it or its Representatives. In the event that either party or any of its Representatives become legally compelled (by deposition, interrogatory, request for documents, subpoena, civil investigative demand or similar process) to disclose any of the Evaluation Material, such party shall provide the other party with prompt prior written notice of such requirement so that the other party may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy and/or waive compliance with the terms of this Agreement. In the event that such protective order or other remedy is not obtained, or that the other party waives compliance with the provisions hereof, the disclosing party agrees to furnish only that portion of the Evaluation Material which it is advised by written opinion of its counsel is legally required and to exercise reasonable efforts to obtain assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded such Evaluation Material.
perturbation.justification: The original text prohibited disclosing information about investigations/negotiations without written consent, while the changed text explicitly allows such disclosure without consent. This directly contradicts the contradicted text which states that Evaluation Material will be kept confidential until a formal agreement is completed. The change introduces a clear conflict, making the confidentiality provision unenforceable and creating an in-text contradiction as defined by the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.

perturbation.original_text: Each party understands and acknowledges that the other party is making no representation or warranty, express or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of the Evaluation Material, and neither the disclosing party, nor any of its respective officers, directors, employees, stockholders, affiliates or agents will have any liability to the other party or any other person resulting from such other party's use of the Evaluation Material. Only those representations or warranties that are made to MDI or Scientific Games, as the case may be, in a Definitive Agreement when, as, and if it is executed, and subject to such limitations and restrictions as may be specified in such Definitive Agreement, will have any legal effect.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party represents and warrants that all Evaluation Material provided is accurate and complete. The disclosing party, its officers, directors, employees, stockholders, affiliates, and agents will be fully liable to the other party for any damages resulting from inaccuracies or omissions in the Evaluation Material, regardless of whether a Definitive Agreement is ever executed. All parts of this text are being altered and thus is being contradicted with what it was stated before.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation creates a direct contradiction regarding representations and warranties. The original text explicitly disclaims any representation or warranty as to the accuracy or completeness of the Evaluation Material and limits liability. The modified text creates an express warranty of accuracy and completeness and imposes full liability, contradicting the disclaimer and limiting liability set in place. It now contradicts the paragraph (7) where the text says 'Each party also agrees that unless and until a Definitive Agreement between the parties with respect to a negotiated transaction has been executed and delivered, neither of the parties nor their stockholders has any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to any such transaction by virtue of this Agreement or any other written or oral expression with respect to such transaction except, in the case of this Agreement, for the matters specifically agreed to herein.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party also agrees that unless and until a Definitive Agreement between the parties with respect to a negotiated transaction has been executed and delivered, neither of the parties nor their stockholders has any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to any such transaction by virtue of this Agreement or any other written or oral expression with respect to such transaction except, in the case of this Agreement, for the matters specifically agreed to herein. For purposes of this paragraph, the term "Definitive Agreement" does not include an executed letter of intent or any other preliminary written agreement, nor does it include any written or verbal acceptance of an offer or bid on the part of either party. Each party further understands that (i) each party shall be free to negotiate for or with respect to any transaction respecting itself or any or all of its business as such party in its sole discretion shall determine (including, without limitation, negotiating with any of prospective merger partners, sellers or buyers 2 and entering into a Definitive Agreement respecting any thereof without prior notice to the other party or any other person) and (ii) each party shall not have any claims whatsoever against the other party, or any of such other party's respective directors, officers, stockholders, affiliates or agents arising out of or relating to any such transaction (other than those as against the parties to a Definitive Agreement with you in accordance with the terms thereof). Neither this paragraph nor any other provision in this Agreement can be waived or amended except by written consent of the party which is sought to be bound, which consent shall specifically make such waiver or amendment.
perturbation.justification: The original text disclaims any representation or warranty, limiting liability regarding the Evaluation Material. The changed text directly contradicts this by creating an express warranty of accuracy and completeness and imposing full liability for inaccuracies or omissions. This directly conflicts with the contradicted text, which states that neither party has any legal obligation until a Definitive Agreement is executed, except for matters specifically agreed to within the agreement. The change introduces a liability regardless of a Definitive Agreement, thus creating a strong contradiction.
